title: Colymbada granatensis
intro: Colymbada granatensis, also known as the Granada rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Colymbada granatensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and supplements, with some of the most common preparations including dried flower and leaf infusions, tinctures, and capsules.
taxonomy: Colymbada granatensis is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Senecioneae. The genus Colymbada is a subset of the larger genus Senecio. Colymbada granatensis is further divided into two subspecies: Colymbada granatensis subsp. granatensis and Colymbada granatensis subsp. sorianoi. Colymbada granatensis is native to the Iberian Peninsula, which is a region in southwestern Europe.
uses: Colymbada granatensis is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Iberian Peninsula. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is also said to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. More research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and benefits.

These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties and antioxidant activity.
